ddls:
CREATE TABLE "size_specifications" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size specification */,
  "size_label" TEXT /* Label for the size (e.g., S, M, L, XL, 2XL) */,
  "bust" REAL /* Bust measurement in inches */,
  "sleeve_length" REAL /* Sleeve length measurement in inches */,
  "mid_length" REAL /* Mid-length measurement in inches */,
  "shoulder" REAL /* Shoulder measurement in inches */,
  "waist" REAL /* Waist measurement in inches */,
  "hip" REAL /* Hip measurement in inches */,
  " inseam" REAL /* Inseam measurement in inches */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group associated with the size (e.g., kids, adults) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender associated with the size (e.g., male, female) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clothing_items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing item */,
  "item_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing item */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the clothing item (e.g., shirts, pants) */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the size specification associated with the item */,
  "style" TEXT /* Style of the clothing item (e.g., casual, formal) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the clothing item */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material used to make the clothing item */,
  "price" TEXT /* Price of the clothing item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clothing_items_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES size_specifications ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clothing item */,
  "stock_level" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the item */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color variant of the clothing item */,
  "variant" TEXT /* Additional variant information (e.g., pattern, style) */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse where the item is stored */,
  "reorder_point" INTEGER /* Reorder point for the item */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Reorder quantity for the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES clothing_items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the warehouse */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the warehouse is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the warehouse is located */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "total" TEXT /* Total amount of the order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order that the item belongs to */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the item ordered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES clothing_items ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)
